result.reasoning: From step_1 of the tool call history, the substance used to test T-cell immune response in the Current Zoology volume 63(5) article on dwarf hamsters is phytohemagglutinin (PHA). From step_4, the International Journal of Food Properties volume 22(1) study tested cooking methods to remove PHA: Soaking + Boiling (12 hours soaking + 30 minutes boiling, total 12.5 hours), Direct Boiling (≥60 minutes boiling), High-Pressure Cooking (15 minutes at 121°C), and Autoclaving (20 minutes at 15 psi). Comparing the times: 15 minutes for high-pressure cooking is shorter than 20 minutes for autoclaving, ≥60 minutes for direct boiling, and 12.5 hours for soaking + boiling. Therefore, high-pressure cooking takes the shortest time. The user question does not provide authoritative casing for the answer, so the sentence-case fallback applies: capitalize the first word of the final answer. Applied casing rule: sentence-case capitalised first word.
